decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas V2 infrastructure over incremental UX polish
decisions.context: Multiple Canvas tickets (DES-164, DES-151, DES-197, DES-196) indicate active UI iteration. With ROM Prototype (ENG-9250) and CSRS stress testing (ENG-10197) in flight, need to decide if Canvas refinement or backend resilience gets Q2 focus.

arguments.for: Backend stability enables Canvas scale-up; ROM prototype could unlock new decision capture modalities. Aligns with 'Build the Moat' pillar.
arguments.against: Canvas UX polish directly impacts adoption metrics. Live Mode (DES-197) could be table-stakes for customer renewals. Nail the Core demands shipping user-visible value.

decisions.title: Commit engineering capacity to Sift Edge performance metrics (ENG-10243) vs. new Edge features
decisions.context: Parquet write duration metrics added. Edge is strategic ('Build the Moat') but unclear if we're instrumenting for optimization or feature velocity. S3 throttling (ENG-9805) suggests scale stress.
decisions.blockedBy: Waiting on Edge usage data from top 10 customers to inform capacity planning

arguments.for: Observability prevents production fires. Metrics = evidence for product decisions. North Star requires reliable data capture.
arguments.against: Metrics don't ship features. Customer asks are for Edge capabilities, not performance dashboards. Risk over-indexing on ops.

decisions.title: Standardize customer co-marketing approach (CAGR-7, CAGR-37, CAGR-22)
decisions.context: Three active customer marketing initiatives (Sophia/Kepler model, Starpath MSA, Modern Day Marine coverage). No clear framework for selecting partners or measuring impact on 'Embed Deeper' pillar.

arguments.for: Structured co-marketing playbook accelerates replication. Clear ROI metrics (pipeline influence, expansion $) inform partner selection. Builds repeatable moat.
arguments.against: Each customer relationship is unique. Premature standardization could constrain high-value one-offs. Marketing team bandwidth already thin.

decisions.title: Sunset Read-Next feature (ENG-10593) or integrate into Explore V2
decisions.context: Explicit removal ticket suggests Read-Next didn't achieve adoption. Need to decide if this represents a failed experiment or a feature needing better placement in Explore V2 information architecture.

arguments.for: Clean removal reduces technical debt, sharpens Explore focus. Failed experiments inform strategy ('Signal > Evidence > Decision'). Team can redirect to higher-impact work.
arguments.against: Premature removal if root cause was discoverability, not value. Could revisit in Explore V2 with better placement. Risk losing minority power users.

keyResults.name: Ask SIFT Query Accuracy (P95 relevance score)

timeAudit.weekSummary: High coordination load from parallel workstreams (Canvas UX, Edge instrumentation, customer marketing). Consider consolidating design reviews or batching co-marketing planning.
